failure_asset.hypothesis_tested: cross-surface failure-mode veto inversion from C0004 state archetype, C0005 analog memory, and C0006 liquidity sweep negative memory after SC0001
failure_asset.next_boundary: next work should open a non-adjacent major campaign such as fold-local supervised edge discovery; do not retry SR0001 by only nudging score gates, opportunity budgets, daily entry counts, stops, targets, holds, or execution filters
failure_asset.reason_not_candidate: fold-isolated MT5 tick evidence completed with 7 losing folds, 2 profitable folds, negative total tick net PnL, and negative proxy profitability even though entries per active day were inside the target range

supporting_lessons.summary: Closed-bar schedule replay matched proxy entries, exits, timestamps, directions, and exit reasons with zero mismatches, so the no-candidate result is not a mechanical parity failure.
Tick execution was worse than closed-bar logic by -4.09 net PnL with 5 worse folds and 4 better folds; tick remains the economic surface.
Cross-surface veto-inversion achieved target activity but did not create fold-isolated tick profitability, so target frequency alone remains insufficient edge evidence.

hypothesis_variant.summary: cross-surface failure-mode veto and inversion synthesis from post-SC0001 negative memory
hypothesis_variant.variant_boundary: SR0001 tests a new cross-family veto-inversion edge surface from C0004 state archetype failures, C0005 analog memory failures, and C0006 liquidity sweep failures; it is not a threshold, window, SL/TP, session, hold, daily-count, or retry nudge.

notes: closed-bar MT5 mode is required for proxy-vs-EA logic parity
tick-mode MT5 is required for execution economics
fold-isolated MT5 tick and execution divergence are required before closeout
proxy evidence is a reference surface only
weak proxy evidence cannot skip MT5 paired validation
daily activity management must use fold-local opportunity budgets, not post-hoc count nudges
mechanical parity compares scheduled entries, directions, exits, exit reasons, and lifecycle behavior
tick-vs-closed-bar differences are execution divergence, not automatic logic failure

feature.summary: closed-bar features spanning archetype instability, analog dispersion, liquidity sweep-reclaim failure state, path quality, session position, and execution-divergence pressure; no feature set selected

label.summary: target-before-stop, adverse-path-avoidance, fold-local consistency, or risk-adjusted label surface remains unrestricted; no label selected

model.summary: fold-local cross-family veto-inversion score, rule, classifier, ranker, or ensemble surface; no model family selected

trade_logic.summary: fixed-lot candidate only when failed-family continuation veto and independent opposite or rejection path-quality evidence align; not a stop, target, session, hold, or threshold nudge
